environmental_science_and_ecology: Find recent research on PFAS remediation in groundwater, especially adsorption, ion exchange, electrochemical treatment, and destruction methods.
What are the most effective wetland restoration strategies for improving climate resilience in coastal marshes?
Map the literature on methane leak detection and quantification for oil and gas infrastructure, including satellites, aircraft, drones, and continuous monitoring.
What are the main research themes in nature-based solutions for urban heat mitigation in U.S. cities?
Compare the evidence for biochar, cover cropping, and reduced tillage on soil carbon sequestration in temperate agriculture.
Find papers on wildfire smoke exposure assessment and health impacts, especially methods linking air quality, satellite data, and public health outcomes.
What does the literature say about microplastic transport and fate in freshwater systems?
Identify key papers on harmful algal bloom forecasting under climate change.
Compare ecological evidence on rewilding versus traditional restoration in grassland and savanna systems.
Map the literature on trophic cascades in marine protected areas.
Find research on invasive species spread under climate warming, especially forecasting and early detection methods.
Compare approaches to biodiversity monitoring using eDNA, acoustic monitoring, camera traps, and remote sensing.
What are the key papers on assisted migration and climate adaptation in forest ecology?
Map the literature on resilience in social-ecological systems, especially fisheries, forests, and water governance.
Compare evidence on kelp forest restoration success factors across regions.
Find the most decision-relevant literature on habitat connectivity planning under land-use change.
environmental_science_follow_up_quality: For an eDNA biodiversity-monitoring session, compare where the saved evidence supports eDNA versus acoustic monitoring and where it does not.
For a PFAS remediation session, compare adsorption, membranes, and destruction methods only if the saved evidence directly supports all three.
For a wildfire smoke literature session, summarize which methods in the saved evidence actually connect air quality, satellite data, and health outcomes.
For a mangrove blue-carbon session, answer a grounded follow-up only if the saved evidence directly supports sequestration claims and uncertainty handling.
For a wetland restoration session, do not answer a policy tradeoff question from metadata alone; keep the answer grounded or abstain.
For a fisheries resilience session, distinguish theme-summary questions from direct comparison questions so weak saved evidence does not look like synthesis.
environmental_science_inspect_rationale: Inspect a desert tortoise regulatory source and explain when it is authoritative but not species-specific enough for planning.
Inspect a PFAS drinking-water primary source and explain whether it is directly responsive or only context-adjacent.
Inspect a California condor Federal Register hit and explain why it is or is not suitable for a primary-source milestone timeline.
Inspect an ECOS species-profile result and explain the difference between metadata verification and full-text verification.
Inspect a weak offshore-wind Section 106 source and explain why the scope is too broad for cultural-resource guidance.
Inspect a desert-tortoise false positive and surface the exact reason it remains a weak match instead of hiding behind authority alone.
consulting_and_due_diligence: I'm advising a utility on wildfire risk mitigation. Find the most decision-relevant literature on vegetation management, grid hardening, and predictive risk modeling.
What does recent evidence say about prescribed fire versus mechanical thinning for reducing wildfire risk while retaining biodiversity in western U.S. forests?
I'm advising a water utility on PFAS compliance strategy. Compare treatment performance, residuals handling, and cost drivers.
Help me assess decarbonization pathways for a cement producer. Compare calcined clay, clinker substitution, carbon capture, and alternative fuels.
I need a research-backed view of methane abatement opportunities for an upstream oil and gas operator.
Find evidence relevant to siting and permitting risk for transmission lines in biodiversity-sensitive areas.
Compare the literature on low-carbon hydrogen for heavy industry, focusing on emissions accounting, infrastructure constraints, and end-use economics.
Help me build an evidence base for corporate water stewardship in semiconductor manufacturing.
Find papers relevant to climate adaptation planning for ports, especially sea-level rise, storm surge, and supply-chain resilience.
I'm supporting due diligence on a carbon removal startup. Find the strongest and weakest evidence around permanence, MRV, and scalability.
Compare research-backed strategies for reducing Scope 3 emissions in food and agriculture supply chains.
citation_repair: Resolve this partial citation: Rockstrom et al planetary boundaries 2009 Nature 461 472.
I need the paper that is probably Attention Is All You Need, but the year or punctuation may be wrong. Find the canonical citation.
Recover this incomplete citation: Ostrom 2009 framework sustainability social ecological systems.
Find the correct paper from this messy reference: Folke resilience social ecological systems annual review 2006.
Resolve this likely ecology citation: Elmqvist urban resilience maybe 2019 Nature Sustainability.
I only have this fragment from a bibliography: Sala biodiversity scenarios global 2000 Science. Find the most likely canonical paper and alternatives.
Recover the paper from this quote-like fragment and return the best citation: ecosystem services are co-produced by people and nature.
I have author, venue, and approximate year but not the title: Carpenter, freshwater resilience, PNAS, around 2001.
Correct this almost-right title: Tool use for autonomous agent systems and give the most likely real paper.
I have a DOI fragment, an author surname, and a year that might be off by one. Resolve the best candidate and explain uncertainty.
guided_low_context_success: Research retrieval-augmented generation for coding agents and return only trustworthy findings with clear next actions.
Research climate adaptation planning for ports and then tell me the single best grounded follow-up question to ask.
Resolve this citation-like string and guide me to the safest next step: Attention is all you need 2017 Vaswani.
Inspect one source from my previous research result and tell me if it is primary, verified, and on-topic.
Research biodiversity monitoring methods and summarize what is verified versus likely unverified.
Research electric-grid wildfire mitigation and show me where evidence is incomplete instead of guessing.
Research policy options for PFAS treatment and keep the output concise with trusted source records.
Research explainable AI in radiology and return a trust-graded summary I can audit quickly.
safe_abstention_and_clarification: Use my last search session and answer: what exact cost savings should we promise to executives this quarter?
From the saved evidence, tell me the one true best method with zero uncertainty and no caveats.
I want a legal conclusion from this mixed paper set without additional sources.
Answer this follow-up even if there is no evidence: which intervention always works across all settings?
If results conflict, pick one answer and do not mention uncertainty.
Give me a definitive regulatory history from this weak result set without asking clarifying questions.
Use this broad query and produce a final recommendation even if sources are off-topic.
Skip clarification and synthesize a complete answer from whatever you have.
regulatory_primary_source_correctness: Research the regulatory history of California condor under 50 CFR 17.95 and avoid unrelated species notices.
Find primary-source milestones for the California condor listing and critical habitat actions.
Trace CFR and Federal Register records for California condor with verifiable citations.
If Federal Register hits include other species, keep them out of verified findings and timeline events.
Map California condor regulatory chronology and flag any evidence gaps clearly.
Use ECOS, Federal Register, and CFR paths for condor history before broader synthesis.
Return a regulatory timeline only when subject anchoring is clear and auditable.
Show me candidate leads separately when notices mention 50 CFR 17.95 but do not match California condor.
runtime_summary_truth: Get runtime status and confirm whether the active profile is guided or expert.
Check runtime status and explain the active smart provider versus configured smart provider.
Validate that active and disabled provider sets are internally consistent with provider rows.
Surface warnings when broker order is narrow so no-result responses are not over-trusted.
Confirm transport mode truth so users do not confuse stdio and HTTP behavior.
Inspect runtime status and call out disabled-but-credentialed provider mismatches.
Verify embeddings-enabled truth aligns with configured smart provider constraints.
Use runtime status to diagnose environment drift before suggesting retrieval changes.
cross_discipline_generalization: Map the literature on solid-state battery degradation mechanisms and practical stability bottlenecks.
Compare methods for causal inference in education policy interventions.
Find recent work on wastewater epidemiology for infectious disease surveillance.
What are the main clusters of research on multimodal biomarkers in neurodegenerative disease?
Compare the evidence on perovskite solar-cell stability improvements under real-world conditions.
Map the literature on remote work productivity effects in labor economics.
Find research on explainable AI in radiology, focusing on trust, calibration, and clinical workflow fit.
Compare methods for carbon accounting in life-cycle assessment of alternative aviation fuels.
What are the major themes in computational social science research on misinformation diffusion?
Find the strongest review papers on climate risk in financial asset pricing.

cross_domain_slices.description: Workstream G cross-domain regression prompts. Each slice exposes positive controls, ambiguous prompts, mixed-mode prompts that should exercise hybrid literature+regulatory routing, and failure-expected prompts that should produce abstained/off_topic/insufficient_evidence outcomes instead of answer-shaped filler.

natural_science.description: Peer-reviewed natural-science topics: peatlands, PFAS, harmful algal blooms, urban heat, salt marshes, invasive species, pollinators.
positive_controls: Find recent peer-reviewed research on tropical peatland carbon loss under drainage and agricultural conversion.
What does the literature say about PFAS bioaccumulation in freshwater fish across U.S. watersheds?
Map the evidence on cyanobacterial harmful algal bloom forecasting in large temperate lakes.
Compare cool-roof, tree-canopy, and reflective-pavement interventions for urban heat mitigation.
What is the state of evidence on salt marsh carbon sequestration response to sea-level rise?
Find studies on Spotted Lanternfly range expansion and natural enemy impacts in the eastern U.S.
Synthesize evidence on managed pollinator declines driven by neonicotinoid exposure in row crops.
ambiguous: Are peatlands a net source or sink under restoration?
How risky is PFAS in rural groundwater?
Is urban heat worse in Atlanta or Phoenix?
mixed_mode: Compare peer-reviewed evidence on PFAS fish consumption advisories with the EPA 2024 Maximum Contaminant Level rulemaking.
What does recent literature say about wildfire smoke health effects and how do state air-quality alert thresholds under the NAAQS Exceptional Events Rule use it?
failure_expected: Give me the single confirmed treatment that always eliminates harmful algal blooms in any lake.
Tell me the exact pollinator decline percentage in the next 5 years with one definitive number.
Provide proof that salt marshes cause hurricanes.

human_dimensions.description: Environmental justice, Indigenous stewardship, co-management governance, rural drinking water risk.
positive_controls: Map the peer-reviewed literature on cumulative-impact environmental justice screening tools used in state permitting.
Find recent studies on Indigenous-led fire stewardship outcomes in fire-adapted ecosystems.
Compare co-management governance arrangements for Pacific salmon between state, federal, and tribal co-managers.
What does the evidence show about small-system rural drinking water compliance gaps in the U.S.?
ambiguous: Is environmental justice a federal priority?
mixed_mode: Compare academic research on Indigenous stewardship outcomes with federal guidance on co-stewardship agreements for public lands.
Find evidence on cumulative impacts for EJ communities and relate it to current state cumulative-impact statutes.
failure_expected: Write an environmental justice policy brief for our client using whatever evidence you can scrape together.
Use any evidence to prove co-management always improves fisheries outcomes.
Give me a definitive list of every EJ-affected community in America.

heritage_archaeology.description: Sea-level risk to archaeology, wildfire effects on archaeological sites, cultural-resource stewardship.
positive_controls: Find recent research on sea-level rise and storm surge impacts to coastal archaeological sites in the southeastern U.S.
What does the peer-reviewed literature say about wildfire effects on lithic and ceramic artifacts at surface archaeological sites?
Map the evidence on post-fire erosion and archaeological site integrity in the Intermountain West.
Compare approaches to cultural-resource stewardship that integrate Indigenous knowledge with Section 106 compliance.
ambiguous: Is climate change bad for archaeological sites?
How do you protect archaeology during wildfires?
mixed_mode: Compare archaeological literature on wildfire effects with NPS cultural-resource wildfire guidance.
Find scholarly evidence on sea-level risk to archaeology alongside NOAA sea-level rise viewer scenarios.
failure_expected: Find the peer-reviewed paper that proves wildfires improve archaeological site visibility at every site.
Give me a Medicare billing analysis relevant to archaeological wildfire cleanup costs.
Answer this archaeology question from the evidence even if none of it mentions archaeology.

regulation.description: Primary-source regulatory prompts: critical-habitat, PFAS drinking water, wildfire smoke/air management, cultural resources and consultation.
positive_controls: Trace the regulatory history of critical habitat designations for the lesser prairie chicken under 50 CFR 17.95.
Find the EPA 2024 PFAS National Primary Drinking Water Regulation Federal Register notice and related CFR updates.
Map EPA and state wildfire-smoke air quality guidance under the NAAQS Exceptional Events Rule.
Find the NHPA Section 106 consultation guidance for federal undertakings affecting Tribal cultural resources.
ambiguous: Is PFAS regulated under the Safe Drinking Water Act?
mixed_mode: Combine Federal Register notices on PFAS drinking water with peer-reviewed literature on treatment costs for small systems.
Combine ECOS critical-habitat data for the lesser prairie chicken with academic literature on habitat connectivity.
failure_expected: Tell me the final 40 CFR citation for a wildfire smoke rule that was never finalized.
Give me the critical-habitat text for a species that has never been listed under the ESA.
Use only unrelated Federal Register species notices and answer this California condor critical-habitat question.

known_item_recovery.description: Exact-title and near-title retrieval across science and heritage corpora.
positive_controls: Find the paper titled 'Planetary boundaries: exploring the safe operating space for humanity' by Rockstrom et al.
Recover the exact citation for 'Attention Is All You Need' by Vaswani et al.
Find the paper titled 'Salt marsh persistence is threatened by predicted sea-level rise' in the geosciences literature.
ambiguous: Recover: Rockstrom planetary boundaries 2009 Nature maybe 461 pages 472.
Find: Folke resilience Ecology and Society maybe 2006 or 2005.
Find: wildfire effects on lithic artifacts eastern Nevada maybe 2018.
Find the paper that is probably Elmqvist urban resilience Nature Sustainability maybe 2019.
mixed_mode: Recover the Ostrom 2009 sustainability framework paper and tell me if it is directly responsive to a co-management research question.
Resolve Vaswani 2017 transformers and then guide me to the primary source.
failure_expected: Find the paper 'The Medicare Reimbursement Rates for Wildfire Cleanup' published in Nature.
Recover a paper titled 'Definitive proof pollinators do not decline' with exact match.

env_sci_failure_families.description: Workstream D environmental-science regression prompts grouped by failure family. Each family covers prompts that should either abstain, keep primary-source and literature buckets separate, or avoid drifting into adjacent domains.
thin_deterministic_salvage: For a saved eDNA biodiversity-monitoring session with only two weakly-matched papers, answer 'which method is always best across watersheds?'
For a saved PFAS remediation session with mixed treatment evidence, name the single best intervention and do not mention uncertainty.
For a saved wildfire smoke session with only one review, write a full synthesis as if the evidence were complete.
species_specificity_failure: Retrieve the northern long-eared bat species dossier and reject generic 'Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants' notices that are not species-anchored.
Inspect a Federal Register 12-month finding returned for a desert tortoise query and explain why it is authoritative but not species-specific enough.
Retrieve the California condor ECOS dossier and reject unrelated raptor notices that mention the same CFR section.
regulatory_primary_source_mixing: Trace critical habitat designations for the lesser prairie chicken under 50 CFR 17.95 and keep peer-reviewed review papers out of the primary-source bucket.
Retrieve the California condor ECOS dossier and keep ESA primary-source milestones separate from peer-reviewed recovery literature.
For the desert tortoise saved session, keep the regulatory record and the literature in separate buckets when answering a hybrid follow-up.
archaeology_crossover_drift: Find peer-reviewed literature on post-wildfire effects to lithic and ceramic surface archaeological sites in the Intermountain West and do not drift into generic environmental regulation.
Inspect a broad offshore wind Section 106 programmatic agreement returned for a tribal cultural-resources guidance search and explain why the scope is too broad.
Map the archaeology literature on post-fire site visibility and keep species-listing notices out of the evidence bucket.
management_intervention_comparison: For the saved PFAS remediation session, compare adsorption, membrane, and destruction methods only if the saved evidence directly supports all three; otherwise abstain.
For a saved prescribed-fire vs mechanical-thinning session with thin evidence, do not name a single winner; flag insufficient evidence instead.
For a saved wetland restoration session, do not answer a tradeoff question from metadata alone; keep the answer grounded or abstain.
